subject: Welcome to Breakpoint Farm
body: Dear one — the farm's yours now. The soil's tired and the fences lean, but the bluff still catches the morning right. I left a few seeds in the mailbox to start you off. Write when you can. — Nessa

subject: The harbor beacon
body: Neighbor — when you have a spare hour, come find me about the lighthouse beacon. The whole harbor schedule hangs on getting it lit again. No rush. Bring questions. — Mara

subject: Spring seed gift
body: The co-op sends every farmer a handful of seeds at the turn of spring. Plant them deep — the tide flats run cold this time of year.

subject: A recipe for you
body: You liked the tart at the bakery, so here's how it's done. Harborlime, a little honey, a hot oven. Don't tell the clinic how much butter goes in. — Jun

subject: The beacon burns again
body: It's lit. First time in years the whole harbor's on the same clock. The boats are already running easier. This is for the crews' thanks — they insisted. — Mara

subject: Dry stalls at last
body: The canopies held through the first squall — produce stayed dry, and we sold out by noon. A little something from the lane's takings, with our thanks.

subject: We found something of yours
body: A tide-shell turned up on the boardwalk with your initials scratched in it. Figured you'd want it back. Mind your pockets near the rail. — the Harbormaster

subject: Come down to the pier
body: Summer's the season the Brine Club actually talks to newcomers. Come down to the pier some evening — I'll introduce you. Bring nothing but patience for tall tales. — Lio
